description_long: This is a vintage, early 20th-century monoplane with a lightweight, skeletal frame primarily constructed from wood and fabric. The aircraft features a single, high-mounted straight wing supported by numerous tension wires and struts, giving it a delicate, open structure. The fuselage is a narrow, truss-like framework with an open cockpit situated just behind the engine and propeller, which are mounted at the very front. The landing gear consists of two large spoked wheels at the front and a small tail skid at the rear, typical of early aviation designs. The tail assembly is distinctive, with a large, angular horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, both supported by additional bracing wires. The overall appearance is reminiscent of pioneering aircraft such as the Blériot XI, emphasizing exposed construction and minimal aerodynamic fairings.
description: A vintage, open-cockpit monoplane with a wooden frame, fabric-covered wings, and exposed bracing wires, featuring a front-mounted propeller and distinctive angular tail surfaces.
description_view_0: Side view showing the open cockpit, front-mounted propeller, large spoked wheels, and the full length of the truss-like fuselage.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the high-mounted straight wing, exposed engine and propeller, and the symmetrical bracing wires.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the skeletal frame, cockpit position, and the angular tail assembly with its supporting wires.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the large, angular horizontal stabilizer, single vertical fin, and the network of bracing wires extending from the tail.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing monoplane with straight, braced wings and no winglets; supported by extensive tension wires and struts.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and large, angular horizontal stabilizer, both braced with wires.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two large fixed main wheels and a rear tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engine and propeller, single-seat with no enclosure.
